demographics.living_situation: lives at home; an adult child is present and helping

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My belly is completely swollen, my skin is yellow, and I keep losing my train of thought.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: His adult child called because over the last day or two he has gone yellow, his stomach has blown up, and he keeps getting confused and drowsy and losing track of what day it is.

symptom_narrative.onset: the yellowing started building up over about a week; the confusion and losing his train of thought came on over the last 48 hours
symptom_narrative.duration: about a week for the yellow skin and swollen belly, getting worse; 2 days of confusion
symptom_narrative.character: skin and the whites of his eyes have turned yellow, his belly is swollen tight and uncomfortable, and he keeps drifting off and losing track of time and conversation
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the family say he is much worse than usual and they are frightened
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: whole belly is swollen; the yellow is all over his skin and in his eyes
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: steadily getting worse, not coming and going
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: it just keeps getting worse on its own
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: the family noticed his hands do a flapping shake when he holds his arms out, and he is very unsteady on his feet
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: he has been unwell from heavy drinking before but the family say he has never been this confused or this yellow
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: he can barely walk because of the swollen belly, he needs help to get around and with washing and dressing, and he is unsteady enough that they are worried he will fall

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: long history of heavy daily drinking
past_medical_history.how_managed: a doctor put him on vitamin tablets for it but he does not take them regularly

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: the family think he was told before that the drinking had started to damage his liver

social_history_volunteered.alcohol: drinks heavily and has done for many years; the family mention there are empty alcohol containers around the house
social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: an adult child is caring for him and is stressed and worried; the home is cluttered and they are worried about him falling

communication_style.typical_message_style: the patient is drowsy and vague, so most of the story is coming from the adult child who is anxious and worried

access_barriers.other: patient is confused and unsteady, so he cannot reliably report his own history or safely get himself to help without assistance

objective_data_offered.value: his skin and the whites of his eyes look yellow
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: family's visual impression over a video call, not a measured bilirubin

objective_data_offered.value: his belly is swollen and tight, and looks bigger than his chest
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: visual impression only; cannot confirm whether the distension is fluid without an examination

objective_data_offered.value: when he holds his arms and hands out straight, his hands do a coarse flapping shake
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: family description of a flapping tremor; not a clinician-elicited sign

objective_data_offered.value: he is drowsy but wakes when spoken to, and he does not know what day or time it is
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: caregiver account of confusion and drowsiness; no formal cognitive assessment

objective_data_offered.value: he is very unsteady and wobbly on his feet and keeps nearly losing his balance
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: family observation of unsteadiness; formal neurological exam not possible
